title: QUIC RFC 9000 DPLPMTUD Probe Tracking Verification
verification_method: Execution and inspection of the helper-backed DPLPMTUD probe-tracking state, requirement-home tests, fuzz coverage, and BenchmarkDotNet Dry validation.
scope: This verification covers the bounded RFC 9000 Section 14.3 DPLPMTUD helper subset: PMTU probe packet-number tracking, acknowledgment/loss outcomes, padding-based probe sizing, BASE_PLPMTU initialization, and per-address-pair MPS isolation. It excludes automatic probe scheduling, packet-builder integration, OS ICMP ingress, alternative-path search, and public API claims.
expected_result: The helper starts each path at QUIC v1's 1200-byte BASE_PLPMTU, tracks only probes larger than the current per-path MPS, promotes MPS only on acknowledgment of the matching address-pair probe, records loss without promotion, computes nonzero PADDING bytes for padding probes with ack-eliciting payload, and keeps path outcomes isolated.
procedure: Run the S14P3 requirement-home tests for positive, negative, edge, and fuzz coverage.
Run the DPLPMTUD BenchmarkDotNet suite with the Dry job to validate the permanent benchmark harness.
Render and check the touched SpecTrace artifacts.
Confirm that no broader PMTU scheduler, packet-builder, socket-ingress, or public-support claim is introduced.
preconditions: The RFC 9000 S14P3 requirement-home tests are discoverable by the test runner.
The benchmark project can execute a filtered BenchmarkDotNet Dry run.

supplemental_sections.heading: Benchmark Scope
supplemental_sections.content: The permanent `QuicDplpmtudStateBenchmarks` suite covers padding-probe acknowledgment, padding-probe loss, and independent per-address-pair probe tracking. The 2026-04-22 Dry run is preserved under `artifacts/benchmark-baseline/20260422-2343-dplpmtud-dry/`; Dry warnings are limited to very small iteration times and should not be interpreted as stable performance numbers.

status_summary: Passed locally for the focused S14P3 requirement-home coverage and the new `QuicDplpmtudStateBenchmarks` Dry suite. The proof remains limited to the helper-backed DPLPMTUD subset and does not claim probe scheduling, packet-builder integration, OS ICMP ingress, alternative-path search, or public API support.
